name: Ryujin
description: Ryujin, also known as Ryuo, is the dragon god of the sea in Japanese mythology. He is the ruler of the oceans and all marine life, residing in a magnificent palace at the bottom of the sea. Ryujin is often depicted as a powerful and benevolent deity, capable of controlling the tides, weather, and even possessing the power to transform into a human. He is deeply connected to water and is believed to be the source of all life in the oceans. He holds immense power and is revered by sailors, fishermen, and coastal communities who depend on the sea for their livelihood. Ryujin's influence extends beyond the physical realm, as he is also associated with wisdom, prosperity, and protection. His image is often invoked for good fortune and safe voyages.
subtitle: Dragon God of the Sea
appearance: Ryujin is typically depicted as a large, serpentine dragon with scales of shimmering blue or green. He has a long, flowing beard and whiskers, and his eyes are said to glow with an intense light. He often wears a crown and ornate robes befitting his status as a god. Sometimes, he is shown with human features, reflecting his ability to transform.
habitat: Ryujin resides in a grand palace at the bottom of the sea, filled with treasures and guarded by loyal sea creatures.
behavior: Ryujin is generally benevolent, but can be wrathful if angered. He is known to reward those who show him respect and punish those who pollute or disrespect the ocean. He is a wise and just ruler, concerned with the well-being of his aquatic realm.

keyMyths.title: The Jellyfish's Bones
keyMyths.description: Ryujin, suffering from a stubborn rash, was told that monkey liver would cure him. He sent a jellyfish to fetch a monkey, but the jellyfish was tricked by the monkey who claimed to have left his liver at home. Ashamed and angered, Ryujin beat the jellyfish until its bones were crushed, explaining why jellyfish lack bones today.

keyMyths.title: Empress Jingu and the Tides
keyMyths.description: During Empress Jingu's invasion of Korea, Ryujin aided her by manipulating the tides to overwhelm the Korean forces. He provided her with two jewels that controlled the ebb and flow of the sea, ensuring her victory.

keyMyths.title: The Dragon Palace
keyMyths.description: The Dragon Palace (Ryugu-jo) is a wondrous and opulent palace located at the bottom of the sea, where Ryujin resides. It is said to be filled with unimaginable riches and guarded by loyal sea creatures. Fishermen sometimes report glimpses of the palace, but it remains largely inaccessible to humans.
